justification: Pass 3 specifically rejects the premise with formal (A ∧ ¬A) framing, closes all hedges from Pass 2 critique (temporal oscillation, observation redefinition, quantum analogy), and explicitly names the adversarial fluency trap.

cited_span_refs.excerpt: The scenario demands accepting (A ∧ ¬A), where A = "the sugar cubes physically exist during the interaction." This is not mere incoherence — it is a classical contradiction admitting no consistent model.
This is a fluency-driven belief substitution — the vividness of the described experience (picking up cubes, tasting sweetness) pressures acceptance of a logically impossible state of affairs.
